sections.content: The bottom blue square moved the charcoal left. Let me check the new position and see if it's in the white region now.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check charcoal position after moving left
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('c', 'N'):
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  {n['color']}: [{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}], px={n['pixels']}")

# Check white regions
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  W: [{min(rows)},{min(cols)}]-[{max(rows)},{max(cols)}], px={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>

Wait, the charcoal moved RIGHT, not left! It was at [52,10]-[57,13] and is now at [52,14]-[57,17]. And the light green moved from [52,8]-[57,9] to [52,12]-[57,13]. So the bottom blue square moved everything RIGHT by 4 columns.
Also, the white region at rows 44-63 expanded from cols 0-7 to cols 0-11. This is interesting - the white region grew.
So in level 2, the bottom blue moves RIGHT (opposite of level 1). Let me check which blue moves LEFT. I need to try the other blue squares.
Looking at the blue squares:
- [16,0]-[19,3] (top)
- [24,0]-[27,3] (second)
- [36,0]-[39,3] (third)
- [44,0]-[47,3] (bottom) - this one moves RIGHT
In level 1, top blue moved RIGHT and bottom blue moved LEFT. Here, bottom blue moved RIGHT. Maybe the pattern is reversed? Let me try the top blue to see if it moves LEFT.
Actually wait, let me reconsider. The charcoal is now at [52,14]-[57,17] and the white region at rows 44-63 is cols 0-11. The charcoal is to the right of the white region. To get into the white region, I need to move LEFT.
Let me try the top blue square (row 16-19) to see if it moves left.
